title: Latent Tyre-State Filter Instead Of Raw Degradation Heuristics
summary: Recent F1 research models tyre degradation as a latent state with pit stops as resets, which matches the competition structure better than fixed compound life constants.
rationale: Recent F1 research models tyre degradation as a latent state with pit stops as resets, which matches the competition structure better than fixed compound life constants. This develops the prior latent-state idea into a concrete, testable direction: infer hidden tyre pace loss online from observed lap-time and degradation signals.
implementation_hint: Per driver-race-stint, fit a simple Kalman/state-space filter or exponentially smoothed hidden state using LapTime_Delta, LapTime (s), Cumulative_Degradation, TyreLife, and reset on PitStop/compound change. Feed only causal summaries such as latent wear level, wear slope, innovation residual, and disagreement between observed pace loss and expected tyre age.
expected_effect: Should capture stint-specific wear better than one-size-fits-all tyre windows, especially when compounds behave differently by circuit or when degradation is nonlinear.
risk: State estimation can overfit if the transition assumptions are too flexible or if noisy laps from traffic/safety effects are treated as pure degradation.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
